page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the arm64 ARMv9 architecture, this instance features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz, with no simultaneous multithreading. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The server does not include local storage or GPU hardware, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate exceptional multi-core performance and memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier status in multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and software compilation with Clang. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 959.17 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 434.92 GB/sec for write operations. However, single-core performance remains average, as demonstrated by middle-tier scores in single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests.

The c8gb.48xlarge offers high resource density and compute efficiency for multi-threaded workloads, though the lack of local NVMe storage requires external storage attachments. The low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2 GB per core represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. This hardware profile is highly suited for highly parallelized workloads, including large-scale software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs at 2.7 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of memory, resulting in a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but delivers a baseline network speed of 200 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show top-tier multi-core capabilities, including high-speed software compilation, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression, alongside exceptional memory read/write bandwidth. Conversely, single-core performance is average. This server is designed for highly parallelized workloads such as static web serving, distributed database operations, and multi-threaded data processing, offering high compute density for parallel tasks despite the lack of local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.48xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server with 192 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 384 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, built for highly parallel workloads.

tagline: A high-density, 192-core arm64 compute platform delivering exceptional multi-threaded performance and high-speed network bandwidth for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores on arm64 architecture
384.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and database operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Suited for highly parallelized workloads, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing
